_comment: Real, generated output of agents/metadata_enrichment/enricher.py's run_enrichment against a FakeDataHubClient seeded to mirror seed/seed_datahub.py, targeting the deliberately under-documented analytics.customer_revenue_summary. This is the proposals.json store core/proposal_engine.py writes: every proposal is PENDING (nothing reaches DataHub until `sentinel proposals accept <id>`), each carries the evidence that grounds it, and loyalty_score got NO proposal — the drafter refused it for insufficient context rather than inventing meaning. Draft text produced with the agent's deterministic test drafter (no ANTHROPIC_API_KEY in the generation environment); the grounding/refusal behavior shown is exactly what the prompt in enricher.py enforces on the live model.

proposals.proposed_value: Per-customer revenue rollup built from analytics.orders_v1 (the canonical orders mart). Consumers query it for top-customer revenue rankings and for revenue joined to customer signup cohorts.
proposals.rationale: Grounded in: upstream analytics.orders_v1 description ('Canonical orders mart'); two sample queries aggregating net_revenue per customer_id

proposals.proposed_value: Customer identifier; joins to raw.customers (used as the join key in observed queries).
proposals.rationale: Grounded in: existing column description; JOIN raw.customers USING (customer_id) in sample query

proposals.proposed_value: Revenue per customer after discounts, in USD; ranked and summed by downstream consumers.
proposals.rationale: Grounded in: ORDER BY net_revenue DESC and SUM(net_revenue) in sample queries; upstream orders mart carries USD amounts

proposals.proposed_value: Count of the customer's completed orders; used as an activity filter (completed_orders > 5) by consumers.
proposals.rationale: Grounded in: WHERE completed_orders > 5 in sample query
